mechanism_narrative: A higher level of the federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raises economy-wide borrowing costs, which reduces equity valuations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points in three months signals deteriorating labor demand, reduces household income expectations, and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances after UNRATE rises more than 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer price inflation and a high federal funds rate create a stagflationary discount mechanism where rising CPI compresses real earnings expectations and elevated FEDFUNDS increases equity discount rates, jointly causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for predicting 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, stronger-than-trend retail sales growth increases the probability of additional Federal Reserve rate hikes, which revalues equities downward and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS above its 6-month trend predicts positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue and earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent from its six-month peak signals contraction in residential investment and downstream consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations following a 10% decline in HOUST from its 6-month peak.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment concurrent with a declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that pressures corporate profit margins through accelerating wage costs and prompts Federal Reserve tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in PAYEMS and decreases in UNRATE fail to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals a negative aggregate demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: A quarterly decline in GDPC1 coincides with positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of release observations.
